title: SOLID, Code Smells, and Evidence-Driven Refactoring · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Evolve a growing circulation system without changing its verified external behavior. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a refactored maintainable service. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign responsibilities by cohesive reason to change rather than forcing one method or one field per class.
Create extension points around observed stable variation without prebuilding speculative plugin systems.
Expose small client-specific roles so consumers do not depend on operations they cannot or should not use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Oop Single Responsibility · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python solid refactoring. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python solid refactoring. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Oop Single Responsibility without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for assign responsibilities by cohesive reason to change rather than forcing one method or one field per class.

content.text: Assign responsibilities by cohesive reason to change rather than forcing one method or one field per class. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python solid refactoring. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Oop Single Responsibility, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a refactored maintainable service while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Single responsibility means every class contains exactly one method. This fails the Oop Single Responsibility contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Typing Documentation (Python 3.14.6) — Controls protocols, generics, class object types, Self, callable typing, annotation introspection, and deprecations.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign responsibilities by cohesive reason to change rather than forcing one method or one field per class.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Safe Refactoring applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refactor through characterization tests, small transformations, reviewable diffs, and unchanged external behavior.
